pages.full_text:
to admire the new flat that Charles' landlord is preparing for him. We had supper at the White Horse with Charles + Jenny + drove on home.

[photo/postcard glued in: Tulips & Roses with tracings made preparatory to embroidery. Drawing by Gertrude Jekyll.]
photo_captions: Tulips & Roses with tracings made preparatory to embroidery. Drawing by Gertrude Jekyll.
pages.notes: This page is a continuation from a previous spread; the text begins mid-sentence ('to admire the new flat...'). No handwritten page number visible at top-left — either absent or cut off in scan. Caption for the postcard/photo is written vertically in the right margin. 'White Horse' is likely a pub name.

pages.full_text:
__Friday 28th June__  2.35.
Simon, Eve, Peter, Nana, Conrad (3½ m) + Tinder get off for Hay on Wye.  We arrive, set up the tent & walk down to the town past the 3 Tuns for a drink.  Headyswith Cider.  As we cross the bridge back to the campsite John Gaskell said "Hello Eve!"  He was out for his evening walk.  He came back to supper.  __Rodnors Farm__***** clean.

Peter & Nana & Conrad slept in the van, Simon & Eve in the tent.

__Saturday__
We walk up to the bookshops, browsed, ate Shepherd's icecreams bought food at the food festival.  At 11 we had coffee with Mrs. Gaskell & John, saw John's mental health allotment.  He gave us 2 lettuces.  We had lunch in the van & napped.  Peter dragged us off to see the map shop before it shut.  We bought maps of the areas of [?Crunachie], [?Swased] & [?Bham] canals  We had cider/beer in a lovely garden &
pages.notes: Handwritten page number at top-right is partially visible but unclear — possibly '32' or similar; recorded as null due to uncertainty. The year cannot be determined from this page alone; volume B covers 2003–2019, and 'Friday 28th June' lands on: 2002 (outside range), 2013, 2019 — 2013 or 2019 are plausible but not confirmed. The time '2.35.' may be a departure time. 'Tinder' appears to be a pet name (dog?). '3 Tuns' is a pub in Hay-on-Wye. 'Rodnors Farm' is a campsite; asterisks likely denote a quality rating. The three canal/area names near the end are difficult to read: '[?Crunachie]' could be 'Monmouthshire' abbreviated or a misread; '[?Swased]' could be 'Severn' or 'Swansea'; '[?Bham]' is most likely 'Birmingham' (canal maps). The text ends mid-sentence — continues on the next spread. 'Headyswith Cider' may be 'Headsy with Cider' or a brand name.
